skill.name: research-synthesizer
skill.description: Synthesizes provided sources neutrally; separates quotes from paraphrase and avoids ungrounded claims.
skill.summary: Synthesizes provided sources neutrally; separates quotes from paraphrase and avoids ungrounded claim...
skill.author: ACSKamloops

security_audit.summary: The static weak cryptographic algorithm finding at SKILL.md:3 is a false positive. The file contains guidance for neutral research synthesis and shows no code execution, network access, filesystem access, or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: SKILL.md:3 contains a plain description of the skill. No cryptographic algorithm, hash function, or security-sensitive implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line is only metadata text about source synthesis. There is no code or cryptographic usage on that line.

content.value_statement: Research reviews often mix evidence, opinion, and unsupported claims. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code summarize provided sources with neutral wording and clear evidence boundaries.
actual_capabilities: Summarizes provided source text in neutral, plain language.
Separates direct quotes from paraphrased material.
Identifies claims that are not supported by the supplied sources.
Calls out uncertainty and evidence gaps explicitly.
Produces concise bullet summaries with next steps for review.
limitations: Does not verify sources that are not provided in the prompt.
Does not replace expert review for legal, medical, or financial research.
Cannot guarantee citation accuracy when source metadata is incomplete.
Does not run searches or fetch external references by itself.

use_cases.title: Summarize Interview Notes
use_cases.description: Turn raw interview excerpts into neutral themes while keeping quotes separate from interpretation.

use_cases.title: Review Source Packets
use_cases.description: Compare supplied articles and identify what each source supports, disputes, or leaves unclear.

use_cases.title: Prepare Evidence Briefs
use_cases.description: Create concise research briefs that distinguish facts, claims, quotes, and open questions.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Source Summary
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you have one source and need a concise neutral summary.
prompt_templates.prompt: Summarize the provided source in neutral language. Separate direct quotes from paraphrase and list any unsupported claims.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Multiple Sources
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when several sources discuss the same topic.
prompt_templates.prompt: Synthesize these sources into shared findings, disagreements, and evidence gaps. Only cite claims supported by the provided text.

prompt_templates.title: Evidence Brief
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when preparing a decision brief from supplied materials.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an evidence brief with key findings, supporting source references, direct quotes, uncertainty, and next review steps.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Claim Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when checking whether a draft is grounded in supplied sources.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this draft against the provided sources. Mark supported claims, unsupported claims, overstatements, quote usage, and needed revisions.

output_examples.input: Three article excerpts about remote work productivity and employee retention.
output_examples.output: The output separates shared findings, disputed points, direct quotes, and evidence gaps without adding outside claims.

output_examples.input: A draft policy memo plus the source notes used to write it.
output_examples.output: The output marks which memo claims are supported, which need citations, and which should be softened.

output_examples.input: Customer interview excerpts about onboarding pain points.
output_examples.output: The output groups themes, preserves notable quotes, and lists follow-up questions for deeper research.

best_practices: Provide complete source text or excerpts with clear labels.
Ask for quote, paraphrase, and uncertainty sections when evidence quality matters.
Include source metadata when you need traceable references.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to invent citations for missing sources.
Do not treat synthesized notes as verified facts without review.
Do not mix trusted sources and unvetted notes without labeling them.

faq.question: Does this skill search the web?
faq.answer: No. It works from sources you provide in the prompt or workspace.

faq.question: Can it create citations?
faq.answer: It can format citation details that you provide. It should not invent missing citation data.

faq.question: How does it handle quotes?
faq.answer: It keeps direct quotes separate from paraphrase so readers can see the evidence type.

faq.question: Can it evaluate conflicting sources?
faq.answer: Yes. It can identify agreements, disagreements, and areas where the supplied evidence is incomplete.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive research?
faq.answer: The skill file contains no code or data transfer behavior. Handle sensitive source text according to your platform rules.

faq.question: What tools support it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
